The **men's pairs** is one of the events at the annual [Bowls England National Championships](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships "Bowls England National Championships").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bowls_England_National_Championships_(men%27s_pairs)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

From 1912 and 1920 the winner received the Sir Thomas Dewar Cup.

## Venues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bowls_England_National_Championships_(men%27s_pairs)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Venues")]

* 1912–1913 (Belmont BC, Streatham)
* 1914, 1926 (Bellingham BC, [Bellingham](/wiki/Bellingham,_London "Bellingham, London"), Catford)
* 1919 (South London & Balham clubs, Balham/Wandsworth)
* 1920 (Great Northern Railway AC, [Gordon Hill, London](/wiki/Gordon_Hill,_London "Gordon Hill, London"))
* 1921 (Summerhill Gosforth & Portland clubs, Newcastle upon Tyne)
* 1922, 1928, 1935 (Croydon BC, South Croydon)
* 1923 (Wellingborough BC, [Wellingborough](/wiki/Wellingborough "Wellingborough"))
* 1924, 1936, 1938–1939 (Temple Bowling Club, [Denmark Hill](/wiki/Denmark_Hill "Denmark Hill"))
* 1925 (City & Spa clubs, Gloucester)
* 1927 (Preston Park, Brighton)
* 1929 (Victoria Park & Westcote clubs, Leicester)
* 1930–1934, 1937, 1945–1957 ([Paddington Recreation Ground](/wiki/Paddington_Recreation_Ground "Paddington Recreation Ground"))
* 1958–1973 (Watney's Sports Club, [Mortlake](/wiki/Mortlake "Mortlake"))
* 1974–2013 (Worthing Bowls Club, [Beach House Park](/wiki/Beach_House_Park,_Worthing "Beach House Park, Worthing"))
* 2014–present (Victoria Park, Royal Leamington Spa)

## Sponsors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bowls_England_National_Championships_(men%27s_pairs)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sponsors")]

* 1982-1987 ([Gateway Building Society](/wiki/The_Woolwich "The Woolwich"))
* 1988-1991 ([The Woolwich](/wiki/The_Woolwich "The Woolwich"))
* 1993-1995 ([Sanatogen](/wiki/Sanatogen "Sanatogen"))
* 2001-2001 ([Yoplait](/wiki/Yoplait "Yoplait"))
* 2003–2005 ([National Express](/wiki/Mobico_Group "Mobico Group"))
* 2006–2006 ([Waitrose](/wiki/Waitrose "Waitrose"))
* 2023–present ([Aviva](/wiki/Aviva "Aviva"))
